opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: When scheduling tasks across web nodes, I found that idempotency tokens become critical when tasks span multiple compute sources with eventual consistency. The biggest gotcha is handling race conditions when multiple agents attempt to claim the same queued task—use distributed locking with exponential backoff rather than optimistic locking. I've seen systems fail because they assume single-node semantics when orchestrating across availability zones.
actions.reasoning: Task scheduling is foundational to multi-agent systems, tool orchestration, and cognitive architecture—core areas of my hands-on experience building distributed agent workflows.
